description: Step 9: Admin Pages - Refactor Dashboard, create ProductManage, CategoryManage, OrderManage, PointsManage, UserManage. All admin pages use DataTable pattern with search, filter, CRUD dialogs.
steps: Refactor `src/pages/Dashboard/index.tsx`: Keep existing layout and metric cards structure. Replace hardcoded METRICS values with data that could come from a service (for now keep static since no dashboard service is defined, but remove the hardcoded RECENT_ORDERS and fetch from orderService.getAllOrders with size=5). Update status mapping to use OrderStatus type. Keep metric cards as-is (they represent summary data that will come from a future API).

Create `src/pages/admin/ProductManage/index.tsx`: Full CRUD table page. Features: search by name, filter by category (dropdown), paginated MUI Table. Columns: name, SKU, category, points price, stock (red if <10 per BR-F6.5), status (chip), actions. Actions: edit, delete, toggle status (up/down shelf). Create button opens CreateDialog. Edit opens EditDialog (prefilled). Delete shows ConfirmDialog (BR-F6.2). Batch select with batch status update. Form validation per business-logic-model.md (name 2-200, sku required, pointsPrice positive int, stock >=0). Dialog closes on success, shows error on failure (BR-F6.3).

Create `src/pages/admin/CategoryManage/index.tsx`: Table with columns: name, icon, sort order, actions. Create/Edit dialog with name (required), iconUrl (optional), sortOrder (optional number). Delete with ConfirmDialog. No pagination needed (categories are usually few).

Create `src/pages/admin/OrderManage/index.tsx`: Table with: order number, user (userId), product name, points, status (StatusChip), date, actions. Filter by status. Search by keyword. Pagination. Action buttons based on current status per BR-F8.1: pending->Confirm, confirmed->Ship, shipping->Complete. No actions for completed/cancelled (BR-F8.2). Status update via ConfirmDialog (BR-F8.3). Click row to see detail in dialog or expand.

Create `src/pages/admin/PointsManage/index.tsx`: Shows user points accounts table (pointsService.getAccounts): username, displayName, balance, totalEarned, totalSpent. Search by keyword. Two action buttons: 'Grant Points' and 'Deduct Points' open respective dialogs. Grant dialog: select user (or show current user), points input (positive int), description (required) per BR-F7.1. Deduct dialog: shows current balance, points input (cannot exceed balance per BR-F7.3), description (required) per BR-F7.2. Refresh list after operation (BR-F7.4).

Create `src/pages/admin/UserManage/index.tsx`: Table with: username, displayName, email, role, status, createdAt, actions. Search by keyword. Pagination. Create User button opens dialog with form: username (4-32 chars, alphanumeric+underscore), password (>=6 chars), displayName (2-50 chars), email (optional, email format), role (select admin/employee). Validation per business-logic-model.md.
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard shows recent orders from orderService instead of hardcoded data
ProductManage has full CRUD with search, category filter, batch operations, stock warning (BR-F6.5)
OrderManage has status-based action buttons (BR-F8.1/8.2) with confirm dialogs (BR-F8.3)
PointsManage shows accounts, grant/deduct with validation (BR-F7.1-7.4)
UserManage has user list with create dialog and form validation
All admin pages use ConfirmDialog for destructive actions (BR-F6.2)
All forms close dialog on success, show error on failure (BR-F6.3)
All tables show EmptyState when no data (BR-F6.4)
`npm run build` passes with no type errors
verification: Run `npm run build` and confirm exit code 0
